Saturninus,    We need to be more careful. I don't know if you noticed, but an old colleague of mine has been dispatched to Bava Nisos. Lutze is a tough nut to crack and has an eye for detail. Keep the shipments separate for now until we can find a better hiding place.    Also, destroy this note, you great oaf.    —H    (You notice the familiar raven symbol at the bottom of the page.)

Saul,    This'll need to be brief, as I believe my life to be at risk. The twins have been growing more and more impatient with my methods. Usually quite tractable, their voices have simmered to a calm eeriness that keeps me upright at all hours...positioned so that the window and door are always in sight. I don't even leave my room without a guard most mornings. I suspect that they're bored with the ritual now, working less fervently to hide their victims' screams from the other villagers than they did at the start. It's a bloodbath.    And that brings me to the villagers... They've finally opened their eyes to the change in tone here. They won't enter any White Mantle structure unless ordered or dragged. They stopped eating our food. A few of them have even managed to escape. It was going so well until now, too; they were enamored with the result of our rebuilding efforts. I suppose too many have gone missing in such rapid succession. It's hard to blame every vanishing on a valravn.    I've tried to slow our movements, but I worry that this has caused a rupture that I cannot fix. The twins are done with me.    I beg of you, come back and control this.    —Rhea

Savant,An individual who claims some preposterous connection with a former colleague of mine recently contacted me, and while his claims concerning that connection are dismissable, he does bring up a good point. Refugees have been streaming into the Black Citadel, Lion's Arch, and Hoelbrak from the northwestern quadrant of the Diessa Plateau and the northeastern quadrant of the Wayfarer Foothills. I don't know what's going on there—and I'm too busy to investigate personally—so, it will have to be you. Go look into it at your earliest convenience. Don't bother reporting back. I trust you to handle the matter with efficiency and style.Good luck, and watch your ears out there.—Zojja

Savant,I wanted to meet you in Lion's Arch, but there's a complication. I just got a letter from a crazy sylvari I know named Caithe, and she wants to speak with me. I know it's annoying, but if you could come, I'd like to have you there. Two heads are better than one. —Zojja

Savant,Rata Sum's abuzz with rumors that a spontaneous bazaar is happening. It's said to be a petri dish of unknowns just waiting to be studied. I'm assigning you to the task, %str1%. Start by heading to Lion's Arch. There you'll find Captain Magnus in the Main Haven Headquarters in Fort Marriner. Magnus is reportedly briefing adventurers about the bazaar. I'll leave the rest in your capable hands.—Zojja

Savant,There's been an incident that you need to address. A butler golem you designed has gone haywire and I need to talk to you about it. The Snaff Prize carries a certain amount of prestige, but there's also responsibility that goes with the title. Come see me right away so we can fix this.—Zojja

Scarlet Briar sought to harness the natural flow of magic for selfish purposes, or so people thought. She orchestrated her reign of terror from the shadows, sending her minions across Tyria to dispose of anyone who stood in her way. But by the time her plans were deciphered, terrible events were already in motion that would change Tyria forever.<c=@Quest>Update from ArenaNet</c>The core path of the Season 1 story is returning! You can begin the journey with Living World Season 1: Flame and Frost in the story journal. While episodes are being restored, this recap episode will remain playable.<c=@Warning>Message from ArenaNet</c>Living World Season 1 was a series of live content events that ran from January 2013 to March 2014. The story was designed to be experienced as it happened. It is currently unavailable for episodic replay like Season 2, so we've created a recap to inform you of the events that bridge the gap between the end of the Personal Story (circa 1325 AE) and Living World Season 2 (1327 AE).Once you've completed the Season 1 recap, we encourage you to peruse the Season 2 catalog of playable episodes. Season 2 episodes are replayable, so once you've unlocked them on your account, you may play them as often as you like, whenever you want. You may enjoy the episodes for free by partying with another player who has them unlocked, or you may purchase them with gems so that you have full access to the associated achievements and rewards.

Scenario: Assassination of Enemy Target    Response Op Designation: Cardinal Blue    Raid based on pinpoint assault tactics using portal technology in combination with fire-based magic and/or weaponry.    Primary risk factor(s): Portal hiccup blowback resulting in friendly fire casualties.    Comment: More testing in a controlled environment required. Lezzi referring to that explosion as a "hiccup" is insensitive, at best, given the entire squad spent six weeks in the infirmary. —Dagda

Scenario: Enemy Occupation of Amnytas    Response Op Designation: Hapless Ladder    Sacrificial ambush stratagem in which enemies are allowed to occupy Amnytas via a staged surrender. Once occupied, all structures are scuttled on the surface of Tyria.    Primary risk factor(s): Results in the complete destruction of Amnytas, the Astral Ward, and the Tyrian impact zone.    Comment: No. And who comes up with these names? —Galrath

Scenario: Missile-Based Attack on Amnytas    Response Op Designation: Cold Frame    Defensive structural redistribution of spire platforms in response to concentrated magic, energy, explosives, or other unknown aerial weapon bombardments.    Primary risk factor(s): Fine control of structures requires (a) staggering magical resources, or (b) total structural retrofit of prototype technology still in R&D phase.    Comment: What does this gain us that a magic-based barrier wouldn't? —Mabon

Scenario: Mounted Aerial Raid of Amnytas    Response Op Designation: Glass Dragon    Rapid response to large-scale aerial raid by Kryptis-possessed winged mounts. Employs experimental weather-control magical technology.    Primary risk factor(s): Indiscriminate targeting also grounds/destroys allied aerial forces; long-term effect on Tyrian weather patterns unclear.    Comment: Intriguing. Investigate current status of weather-control technology/magic. —Isgarren

Scenario: Multipoint Rift-Based Amnytas Incursion    Response Op Designation: Mosaic Wrath    Defensive/counteroffensive deployment of magically charged sentient amalgamation requiring multiple arrayed summoners in staged coordination.    Primary risk factor(s): Magic consumption requires years or decades of siphoning; defenselessness of multiple critical ward members.    Comment: Too much time, too much magic, leaves us vulnerable. And I'd need every ounce of power Lyhr could muster. Absolute last resort. —Dagda

Scenario: Possession-Enhanced Intelligence Gathering    Response Op Designation: Rude Virus    Counterintelligence initiative allowing Kryptis possession of Astral Ward members to disseminate misinformation to enemy strategists and create tactically favorable combat scenarios.    Primary risk factor(s): Participation involuntary—ineffective if subject has awareness of purposeful possession; possible full Kryptis possession and/or death of ward subject.    Comment: Risky. And morally gray. At best. Further research needed. —Isgarren
